title: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
content: The 54 firm-strong group, which is backed by the City of London Corporation, will spend the next year working on live tokenisation use cases across UK financial markets.
summary: Major financial institutions like BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley have joined a UK government-backed taskforce. This 54-firm group will spend the next year developing and implementing live tokenization use cases across the UK's financial markets.

title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
content: A Treasury-backed report targets putting repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, and warms to permissionless networks that firms like Ripple have been building on.
summary: A UK Treasury-backed report outlines plans to tokenize financial instruments like repo, gilts, and funds, aiming for on-chain implementation within two years. The report expresses support for permissionless networks, referencing firms like Ripple as examples of companies utilizing this technology.

title: Robinhood Chain surges into top five by DEX volume: Bernstein
content: Robinhood's new blockchain quickly became a top-five network by DEX volume, signaling strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform, the broker said.
summary: Robinhood's new blockchain has quickly ascended to become one of the top five networks by decentralized exchange (DEX) volume. This rapid growth suggests strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform.

title: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative pivots to Solana for tokenization, stablecoin issuance
content: The SBI Solana Global joint venture now includes the Solana Foundation, the Swiss organization that oversees the layer-1 network.
summary: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative is pivoting to Solana for tokenization and stablecoin issuance. Their SBI Solana Global joint venture has also expanded to include the Solana Foundation.

title: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities send bitcoin lower even as ETF flows show demand
content: Your day-ahead look for July 13, 2026
summary: Bitcoin prices saw a downturn following renewed hostilities between the U.S. and Iran, despite strong underlying demand indicated by ETF flows. This report provides a forward-looking market analysis for July 13, 2026.

title: Paradigm shifts vs bubbles: AI chips and bitcoin show powerful trends can still produce severe corrections
content: Structural change can create lasting opportunities, but explosive rallies in semiconductors, metals and bitcoin show how quickly strong narratives can become speculative excess.
summary: The article discusses how powerful structural trends, exemplified by AI chips and Bitcoin, can create lasting opportunities but are also prone to severe market corrections. It highlights the risk of strong narratives quickly turning into speculative excess, leading to explosive rallies and subsequent deflations.

title: Stablecoin market cap has shrunk by $10 billion since May, but analyst sees no reason to panic
content: The market shrank by $7.7 billion in June alone, the largest dollar amount since May 2022's Terra-Luna crash, but stablecoins will likely resume their long-term growth, one analyst said.
summary: The stablecoin market cap has shrunk by $10 billion since May, with June alone seeing a $7.7 billion reduction, marking the largest monthly drop since the May 2022 Terra-Luna crash. Despite this significant decrease, one analyst maintains there is no cause for panic, forecasting a resumption of long-term growth for stablecoins.

title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
content: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse says he and co-founder Chris Larsen considered winding the company down and handing its XRP to shareholders before deciding to fight the 2020 lawsuit.
summary: Ripple's CEO Brad Garlinghouse revealed that he and co-founder Chris Larsen once considered winding down the company and distributing its XRP holdings to shareholders. This significant strategic deliberation occurred before they ultimately decided to fight the 2020 lawsuit, offering insight into their past considerations.

title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
content: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Michael Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.
summary: The Bitcoin BIP 110 proposal, aimed at capping arbitrary data for a year, is nearing its deadline with no miner support. Key figures like Michael Saylor and Adam Back warn that turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create greater risks for the network.

title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
content: Bonzo Lend lost approximately $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract on the Hedera network.
summary: The lending protocol Bonzo Lend suffered a substantial loss of approximately $9.05 million due to an exploit on the Hedera network. An attacker leveraged a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract, causing Bonzo Lend's total value locked to plummet by 77%.

title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
content: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.
summary: The crypto IPO market is experiencing a stall due to funding constraints and investor caution, with capital instead rotating to AI and macro uncertainty. Christian Lopez of Cohen & Company Capital Markets attributes the delay to these factors rather than regulation. This shift in capital highlights a notable trend in investor interest.

title: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
content: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.
summary: According to Wirex CEO Chet Shah, the UK is finally demonstrating a serious approach to cryptocurrency through several recent regulatory steps. This shift indicates that the UK government may be moving past its previous indecisiveness regarding crypto policy.

title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
content: It's a sign of the times as the troubled company swaps its bitcoin treasury ambitions for AI data centers.
summary: Empery Digital, a company focused on Bitcoin treasury, has sold roughly half of its BTC holdings. This strategic shift indicates the troubled company is pivoting its ambitions from cryptocurrency to investing in AI data centers.

title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
content: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.
summary: The Ethereum Foundation deployed coordinated AI agents to scan its validator software, leading to the discovery of a remotely triggerable crash bug. While the AI successfully identified this critical vulnerability, human intervention was necessary to confirm its authenticity, as the AI agents also generated a significant number of false positive findings. This highlights both the promise and current limitations of AI in blockchain security auditing.

title: Coinbase Ventures tops crypto VC list for H1 2026
content: Coinbase’s venture capital arm leads 2026 crypto investing even as funding withered and unique investors shied away amid the bear market.
summary: Coinbase Ventures has been identified as the leading venture capital investor in the crypto sector for the first half of 2026. This achievement occurred despite a challenging bear market, which saw overall funding decline and fewer unique investors engaging in the space.

title: UK tokenization push could add as much as $44B to annual output by 2035: Report
content: The government-backed roadmap calls for the UK’s first digital gilt by early 2027 and seeks to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing.
summary: The UK government has announced a plan to tokenize assets, aiming to release its first digital gilt by 2027, which could increase annual output by $44B by 2035. This move is expected to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing. The plan is part of a broader effort to modernize the UK's financial system.

title: Bitcoin whale moves $188M for first time in 7 years
content: A dormant whale transferred BTC worth $188 million after seven years of holding, adding to the growing ratio of whale transfers to cryptocurrency exchanges.
summary: A dormant Bitcoin whale has transferred $188 million worth of BTC for the first time in 7 years, contributing to the increasing trend of whale transfers to cryptocurrency exchanges. This move may indicate a significant shift in market dynamics. The transfer's impact on the market remains to be seen.

title: Binance June futures volume at $1.6T defies crypto spot trading slump
content: Binance futures volume jumped 80% in June, to $1.61 trillion, outpacing rivals while broader crypto trading activity remained weak.
summary: Binance futures volume increased by 80% in June to $1.61 trillion, despite a slump in broader crypto spot trading activity. This growth outpaced its rivals, showcasing a strong performance for Binance. The surge in futures volume indicates a significant trend in the crypto market.

title: BTC price bull market to begin in September? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
content: Bitcoin price analysis called for the end of the bear market within three months as the US-Iran war sent BTC lower from strengthening $64,000 resistance.
summary: Bitcoin price analysis predicts the end of the bear market within three months, with a potential bull market starting in September. The US-Iran war caused a dip in BTC price after reaching $64,000 resistance. The analysis suggests a potential upside for Bitcoin in the coming months.

title: Japan stablecoin payments advance with Lawson trial, Netstars launch
content: Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo as Netstars launches a merchant service supporting USDC, USDT and JPYC.
summary: Japan's Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo, while Netstars is launching a merchant service that supports USDC, USDT, and JPYC. This move marks a significant advancement in stablecoin payments in the country. The trial and launch aim to increase the adoption of cryptocurrency in everyday transactions.

title: AI microbusinesses could drive $262B in stablecoin volume by 2033: Swyftx
content: The AI-native cohort of the expanding gig economy could increasingly use stablecoins to avoid slow and expensive traditional payment rails, Australian crypto exchange Swyftx said.
summary: Australian crypto exchange Swyftx predicts that AI microbusinesses could drive $262 billion in stablecoin volume by 2033. This growth is anticipated as the AI-native gig economy increasingly adopts stablecoins to bypass slow and expensive traditional payment systems.

title: Bitcoin ETFs draw $197M, snap 8-week outflow streak
content: Analysts are not yet ready to call it a recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.
summary: Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds attracted $197 million in inflows, breaking an eight-week streak of outflows. Despite this positive movement, analysts remain hesitant to declare a full recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.

title: Robinhood L2 sparks ETH optimism, Saylor 'muddies waters.' Hodler's Digest, July 5-12, 2026
content: Even Ethereum's critics say Robinhood Chain is bullish for ETH. Nigel Farage and US President Donald Trump become embroiled in crypto related scandals, and more.
summary: Robinhood Chain, an L2 solution, is sparking optimism for Ethereum, with even critics acknowledging its bullish impact on ETH. This development coincides with political figures Nigel Farage and Donald Trump becoming involved in crypto-related scandals.

title: Strategy's Saylor needs clarity in BTC pivot message to convince investors: StanChart
content: Standard Chartered sees communication challenges facing the biggest digital asset treasury company as "muddying the waters" for Bitcoin in the near term.
summary: Standard Chartered believes communication challenges from the largest digital asset treasury company, implied to be MicroStrategy and Michael Saylor, are creating near-term uncertainty for Bitcoin. The bank suggests Saylor needs to provide more clarity in his Bitcoin pivot message to reassure investors.

title: Empery Digital shares rise after selling BTC to fund AI data center project
content: The sales come months after a major Empery shareholder demanded the firm ditch its Bitcoin treasury strategy and sought the resignation of its CEO and board.
summary: Empery Digital's shares increased after the company sold its Bitcoin treasury, a move intended to fund an AI data center project. This strategic pivot follows a major shareholder's demand for the firm to abandon its Bitcoin strategy.

title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
content: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.
summary: Bonzo Lend suffered a $9 million loss due to an oracle exploit on the Hedera network. An attacker exploited a flaw in Supra's on-chain oracle verifier to inflate the value of SAUCE collateral and borrow funds.

title: Dollar stablecoins could improve FX access but amplify currency runs: IMF
content: An IMF working paper says US dollar stablecoins can improve access to foreign currency but may also help coordinate exits from local currencies during periods of severe exchange-rate stress.
summary: An IMF working paper suggests that dollar stablecoins could facilitate access to foreign currency, particularly in emerging markets. However, the paper also warns that these stablecoins might exacerbate currency runs by coordinating exits from local currencies during periods of significant exchange-rate instability.

title: Robinhood says its AI agent feature will ‘soon’ be assisting crypto traders
content: More than 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by Robinhood equities and options traders since late May, when Robinhood launched a beta version of the product.
summary: Robinhood announced that its AI agent feature will soon be available to assist crypto traders, building on its successful beta launch in late May. Over 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by equities and options traders since the product's initial release.

title: Kraken to overhaul app with AI investing assistant
content: The redesigned app will use AI to recommend trades and tailor investing tools around users' financial goals as Kraken expands into broader financial services.
summary: Kraken is redesigning its app to include an AI investing assistant that will provide users with personalized trade recommendations and tailored investing tools based on their financial goals. This overhaul is part of Kraken's expansion into broader financial services. The new app aims to enhance user experience and investment decisions.

title: Senate Democrats call for hearings into Trump’s ties to crypto amid CLARITY Act discussions
content: Five senators called for committee hearings to investigate US President Donald Trump’s policies potentially being influenced by crypto funding from United Arab Emirates-linked and other entities.
summary: Senate Democrats are calling for hearings to investigate if President Trump's policies are being influenced by crypto funding from entities linked to the United Arab Emirates. This comes amid discussions of the CLARITY Act. The investigation aims to shed light on potential ties between Trump and crypto donors.

title: US CBDC ban to go into effect without Trump signoff on housing bill
content: Donald Trump confirmed on social media that he would not sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a ban on a US CBDC until the end of 2030, setting the stage for the bill to become law on Saturday.
summary: The US is set to ban a central bank digital currency (CBDC) until 2030 as President Trump refuses to sign a housing bill containing the ban, allowing it to become law. The ban is expected to go into effect, shaping the country's stance on digital currency. This decision may impact the development of digital currency in the US.

title: Backpack joins race for 24/7 stock markets with tokenized equities
content: The launch comes as tokenized stocks become one of the fastest-growing corners of crypto, with exchanges and traditional finance firms racing to expand 24/7 trading.
summary: Backpack has entered the competition to provide 24/7 stock markets using tokenized equities. This move comes as tokenized stocks are rapidly growing within the crypto space, leading both traditional finance firms and exchanges to expand their 24/7 trading offerings.

title: Japan’s largest security token platform moves nearly $3 billion to Avalanche blockchain
content: Progmat develops security token infrastructure that accounts for 64.6% of Japan’s security token market total issuance value.
summary: Progmat, Japan's largest security token platform, has moved nearly $3 billion to the Avalanche blockchain, holding 64.6% of Japan's security token market total issuance value. This move signifies a major shift in the country's security token infrastructure. The transition underscores the growing importance of blockchain technology in Japan's financial sector.

title: Tom Lee says users ‘starting to see Ethereum as money’ as Bitmine adds 27,801 ETH
content: Bitmine accumulates 5.77M ETH tokens, 4.8% of supply, as Tom Lee cites Robinhood Chain driving everyday user adoption.
summary: Tom Lee notes that users are starting to view Ethereum as a form of money, driven by the everyday adoption spurred by Robinhood Chain. Bitmine has accumulated 5.77M ETH tokens, representing 4.8% of the total supply, with a recent addition of 27,801 ETH. This growth indicates a significant interest in Ethereum.

title: Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains: Bernstein
content: Robinhood Chain hit $3.1B in weekly DEX volume and 65K users since its July 1 launch, as the Layer 2 eyes tokenized markets, Bernstein said.
summary: Robinhood Chain has reached $3.1 billion in weekly DEX volume and 65,000 users since its launch on July 1, according to Bernstein. The Layer 2 chain is eyeing tokenized markets. This milestone places Robinhood Chain among the top five chains.

title: Strategy sells $467 million in MSTR shares, makes no bitcoin purchases as USD reserve hits $3 billion
content: Strategy's total holdings remain at 843,775 BTC and account for around 4% of the 21 million bitcoin supply cap — worth roughly $53 billion.
summary: Strategy sold $467 million in MSTR shares and has a USD reserve of $3 billion, with no new bitcoin purchases, while holding 843,775 BTC worth $53 billion. The company's bitcoin holdings account for around 4% of the total bitcoin supply cap. This move indicates a significant shift in Strategy's asset management.

title: SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build Japan-based onchain financial market
content: SBI and the Solana Foundation are collaborating to build an onchain financial market with SBI R3 rebranding to SBI Solana Global.
summary: SBI Holdings is partnering with the Solana Foundation to build a Japan-based on-chain financial market, with SBI R3 being rebranded to SBI Solana Global. This collaboration aims to create a new financial market infrastructure. The partnership marks a significant development in the adoption of blockchain technology in Japan's financial sector.

title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
content: The central bank and the SEC have reportedly begun using data analytics tools to examine abnormal stablecoin trades, with a focus on USDT.
summary: The Bank of Thailand and the SEC are auditing high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance, using data analytics tools with a focus on USDT. This effort aims to regulate and monitor stablecoin transactions. The move is part of a broader initiative to prevent financial crimes.

title: Japan’s Lawson convenience store pilots stablecoin payments with JPYC: report
content: This is part of a proof-of-concept involving telecommunications giant KDDI and crypto wallet firm HashPort.
summary: Japan's Lawson convenience store chain is piloting stablecoin payments using JPYC in a proof-of-concept. This initiative involves collaborations with telecommunications giant KDDI and crypto wallet firm HashPort.

title: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto
content: The Japanese financial giant's recent crypto deals offer insights into how traditional finance is approaching digital assets.
summary: Japan's financial giant, SBI Holdings, is significantly investing in the crypto space. These recent deals offer valuable insights into how traditional finance institutions are evaluating and engaging with digital assets.

title: Michael Saylor posts cryptic Strategy chart after $216 million bitcoin sale: ‘Orange dots tell only part of the story’
content: Strategy holds 843,775 BTC at an average cost of $75,476, leaving the position roughly $9.7 billion underwater with bitcoin trading near $64,000.
summary: Michael Saylor's company, Strategy, holds 843,775 BTC at an average cost of $75,476, currently placing their position roughly $9.7 billion underwater with Bitcoin trading near $64,000. This news follows a $216 million Bitcoin sale by the company, after which Saylor posted a cryptic chart suggesting there's more to the story than just the "orange dots."

title: Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend hit for $9 million after Supra verifier accepts manipulated price update
content: A second wallet borrowed another roughly $1 million but identified itself as a white hat hacker and said the funds would be returned.
summary: The Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend was exploited for $9 million due to a manipulated price update accepted by a Supra verifier. A second wallet borrowed $1 million but claimed to be a white hat hacker and promised to return the funds. The incident highlights a security vulnerability in the protocol.

title: Housing bill that includes a CBDC ban passed into law without Trump’s signature
content: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a provision that bans the Fed from issuing a CBDC, has become law.
summary: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which contains a provision banning the Federal Reserve from issuing a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), has officially become law. This legislation was enacted without former President Trump's signature, marking a significant development regarding digital currency policy in the United States.

title: DOJ to drop charges against man accused in $722 million BitClub crypto Ponzi scheme: Bloomberg
content: A former contestant on 'The Apprentice' is one of the persons urging the DOJ to drop the charges, the report said.
summary: The US Department of Justice is dropping charges against a man accused of involvement in a $722 million cryptocurrency Ponzi scheme called BitClub. A former contestant on 'The Apprentice' reportedly urged the DOJ to drop the charges. The scheme's total value is significant, indicating a major case in the crypto space.

title: Here’s what’s next for the Clarity Act as Congress returns to Washington
content: Congress returns to Washington next week, ready to take up cryptocurrency legislation before the midterm election calendar takes over.
summary: Congress is returning to Washington and will take up cryptocurrency legislation before the midterm elections. The legislation is expected to address various aspects of cryptocurrency. The return of Congress may bring significant changes to the crypto space.

title: SK Hynix’s $26.5 billion US listing brought to Telegram users via xStocks
content: Solana said that tokenized SK Hynix shares on SOL can be acquired through Backpack, xStocks and Ondo Finance.
summary: Tokenized shares of SK Hynix, linked to a $26.5 billion US listing, are now accessible on the Solana blockchain. These digital assets can be acquired by users through platforms such as Backpack, xStocks, and Ondo Finance.

title: Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
Strategy (MSTR) raised $467 million through stock sales to boost its cash reserve to $3 billion while leaving its 843,775 BTC holdings unchanged for a second straight week.
This post Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strategy (MSTR) has raised $467 million in cash through stock sales, increasing its cash reserve to $3 billion, while keeping its large holding of 843,775 Bitcoin unchanged. This move indicates the company's confidence in its Bitcoin holdings. The funds will likely be used to further the company's business operations.

title: U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
Rep. French Hill urged the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act before the August recess, arguing the bill would establish a transparent crypto market framework that addresses ethics concerns surrounding President Trump's digital asset ventures.
This post U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: US Representatives are urging the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act in July, which aims to establish a transparent crypto market framework and address ethics concerns. The bill is seen as a way to provide clarity on digital asset regulation. Rep. French Hill argues that passing the bill before the August recess is crucial.

title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.
This post Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Circle has received final approval from the OCC to establish a national trust bank, enabling it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expand its digital asset custody business. This move allows for greater regulatory oversight and stability. Circle's national trust bank will operate under federal supervision.

title: Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan
Metaplanet is taking its Bitcoin strategy beyond treasury accumulation, launching a study to explore tokenized, Bitcoin-backed credit products that could reshape Japan's debt market.
This post Metaplanet Announces Joint Study to Bring Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit to Japan first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Metaplanet is launching a joint study to introduce Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan, aiming to transform the country's debt market. This initiative goes beyond Metaplanet's existing Bitcoin accumulation strategy. The study will explore tokenized credit products backed by Bitcoin.

title: Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
Standard Chartered reaffirmed its $100,000 end-2026 Bitcoin price target, calling Bitcoin "a screaming buy" and arguing that Strategy's recent sell-off reflects a communication issue around its new monetization strategy rather than a weakening Bitcoin outlook.
This post Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Standard Chartered has reaffirmed its $100,000 Bitcoin price target by the end of 2026, calling it a 'screaming buy'. The company attributes Strategy's recent sell-off to a communication issue, not a weakening Bitcoin outlook. This suggests a positive outlook for Bitcoin's future value.

title: JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains
JPMorgan says Strategy's Bitcoin sales are a short-term concern, but the bigger long-term risk is that banks and institutions adopt private blockchains over public networks, reducing activity and capital flows across the broader crypto ecosystem.
This post JPMorgan Says the Real Threat to Bitcoin Isn’t Strategy (MSTR) — It’s Private Blockchains first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: JPMorgan believes the real long-term threat to Bitcoin is not Strategy's Bitcoin sales, but rather the adoption of private blockchains by banks and institutions, which could reduce activity and capital flows in the crypto ecosystem. This could have a significant impact on the broader crypto market. The institution views this as a bigger risk than short-term sales.

title: Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test
BitcoinTreasuries.net says June marked the first major stress test for Bitcoin-backed preferred shares, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the growing corporate Bitcoin financing model.
This post Bitcoin’s New Debt Machine is Facing Its First Major Test first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin-backed preferred shares faced their first major stress test in June, with Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA rebounding after a sharp sell-off, reinforcing confidence in the corporate Bitcoin financing model. This test is significant for the growing Bitcoin financing sector. The outcome has reinforced confidence in the model.

title: New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond
New Hampshire's Executive Council voted 3-2 to reject a proposed $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, shelving what supporters said would have been the world's first such issuance despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers.
This post New Hampshire Council Rejects $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: New Hampshire's Executive Council rejected a $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, which would have been the world's first such issuance. The proposal was voted down 3-2, despite arguments that it posed no risk to taxpayers. The rejection shelves a potentially groundbreaking financial instrument.

title: BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody
BitGo launched new quantum-risk tools that help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets.
This post BitGo Adds Quantum-Risk Controls to Bitcoin Custody first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: BitGo has launched new tools to help institutional Bitcoin holders identify and reduce potential future quantum-computing exposure in their custody wallets. This move aims to mitigate the risks associated with quantum computing for Bitcoin custody. The tools will help holders protect their assets from potential quantum-related threats.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
