This discovery sheds light on how these models think and understand information. The finding is significant for the development of AI technology.

title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
content: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…
summary: Anthropic developed a technique to better understand large language models, finding a range of results from mundane to unnerving. The tool, called the Jacobian lens, provides insight into the models' thought processes. This discovery could significantly impact AI development.

title: EmTech AI 2026: The Rise of the AI Platform
summary: The EmTech AI 2026 conference discusses the growing importance of AI platforms, highlighting their potential to transform industries and revolutionize the way we live and work. The conference explores the latest advancements in AI technology and its applications. AI platforms are expected to play a crucial role in shaping the future of various sectors.

title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI is back in the spotlight, with reports that he is discussing giving…
summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman proposes that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI, with a potential $300 stake for families. This idea is being discussed, sparking interest in the tech community. The Treasury also issued an AI warning, highlighting the significance of AI's impact.

title: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
content: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six months into the future. Returning to the foundational elements of AI architecture—the…
summary: The article discusses the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the importance of understanding the foundational elements of AI architecture for IT leaders to scale and make valuable investments. It highlights the evolution of AI and the introduction of risk, emphasizing the need for a solid foundation. The article aims to guide IT leaders in navigating the growing technology.

title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
content: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that the company stole trade secrets, with senior leadership, including a former Apple employee, involved in the misconduct. The lawsuit claims that OpenAI's actions were intentional and directed by its top officials. This case has significant implications for the tech industry.

title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue states that open source AI is booming, with the company becoming a hub for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, now used by half of the Fortune 500. This growth indicates a significant shift in the AI industry. Hugging Face has become a key player in the open source AI space.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
content: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.
summary: SK Hynix has raised $26.5B in the largest foreign IPO in US history, driven by the AI chip boom. The company, along with Samsung, is being urged to build new factories in the US. This development highlights the growing importance of AI chips and the need for domestic manufacturing.

title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
content: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]
summary: Hugging Face's CEO, Clem Delangue, states that open source AI is booming, with the company serving as a platform for AI builders to share and download models and datasets, used by half of the Fortune 500. Companies are shifting away from renting AI and towards open source solutions. This trend indicates a significant change in the AI industry.

title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.
summary: OpenAI's new models, including GPT 5.6, will continue to power Microsoft's workplace and productivity apps, amid speculation of a potential breakup between the two companies. This ensures continuity for Microsoft's Copilot 365. The partnership remains intact for now.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
content: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 executive, is stepping down from her role after a longer-than-expected medical leave, creating a leadership vacuum at a crucial time for the company. OpenAI is considering an IPO and competing with Anthropic in the enterprise market. This change may impact OpenAI's strategic direction and growth plans.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
content: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.
summary: OpenAI has launched a new family of models, GPT-5.6, which promises improvements in various areas, including cybersecurity. The new models are expected to enhance performance and capabilities. This launch is a significant update from OpenAI.

title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
content: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.
summary: Lyzr, a startup building AI agents, used its own AI agent to raise $100 million, demonstrating the product's effectiveness. This achievement showcases the potential of AI in handling complex tasks, such as fundraising. The successful raise highlights the viability of AI agents in enterprise settings.

title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
content: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.
summary: OpenAI is shutting down its AI-powered browser Atlas after less than a year, but will integrate some features into its desktop app and a Chrome extension. This move indicates a shift in OpenAI's browser ambitions. The company is re strategizing its approach to AI-powered browsing.

title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
content: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.
summary: Elon Musk has expressed support for Mythos/Fable and promised not to limit Anthropic's access to its models, which could impact the company's $40 billion revenue. This statement aims to reassure Anthropic amidst potential concerns. Musk's assurance is significant for Anthropic's future

title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
content: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.
summary: The AI ROI debate has resurfaced with significantly larger numbers and potential consequences, prompting a discussion on the value of AI with a $3 trillion question. This debate may have substantial implications for the future of AI development and investment. The question is whether AI can provide a worthwhile return on investment.

title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
content: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.
summary: Meta has launched Muse Spark 1.1, an AI coding tool that can handle large workloads, fix bugs, and assist with code migrations, targeting enterprise clients. This move marks Meta's entry into the competitive AI coding market. The tool aims to provide automation solutions that enterprises increasingly need.

title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
content: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
summary: OpenAI is accused of hiding evidence in a copyright trial related to ChatGPT, with news publishers claiming the company concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The lawsuit has been escalated with a new motion for sanctions. This development may have significant implications for AI copyright laws.

title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
content: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.
summary: Google will now require advertisers to disclose when their ads use artificial intelligence to create synthetic or digitally altered content, extending a policy previously only applied to election ads. This change aims to increase transparency in online advertising. The move is part of Google's efforts to prevent misleading ads.

title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
content: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."
summary: Gradium, a Paris-based AI voice startup, has raised $100M in seed funding backed by Nvidia. The company plans to use the funds to open a new office in the Bay Area and compete for top talent. This move aims to strengthen its position in the global AI ecosystem.

title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
content: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."
summary: The government's decision to release OpenAI's frontier model is unclear, with details of the dialogue between the government and OpenAI and Anthropic not being disclosed. The decision-making process behind this release is still shrouded in mystery. This has raised questions about the safety and implications of releasing such models.

title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
content:
Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. 
The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering Face ID, Animoji, and  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple's failed self-driving car program unexpectedly led to the development of the company's powerful on-device AI chips. The necessity for robust AI processing for the car project resulted in the creation of the Neural Engine. This crucial technology, first seen in the A11 Bionic and iPhone X, now underpins Apple's AI capabilities for features like FaceID.

title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
content:
A yard sign opposing a planned data center is displayed along Route 54 in Mount Carmel Township Northumberland County. | Image: Getty Images	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
Years before the AI boom threatened local power grids, a small group of protesters set the stage for the battles cropping up across communities today. In 2015, Apple announced plans to build a roughly $1 billion data center in the sleepy town of Athenry, Ireland. The data center's 500-acre site would power Apple's services in Europe, including iTunes, iMessage, and Siri. At th …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The article highlights the increasing opposition to data centers, particularly those supporting the AI boom, due to concerns about their impact on local power grids and communities. It traces these disputes from early protests against an Apple data center in Ireland in 2015 to current battles in various locations.

title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
content:
Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission.
"Earlier this week, we announced that one way for people to generate images in Meta AI is by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they want to reference," Meta says in an update to a blog post about its new Muse Image AI model. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give pe …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Meta is shutting down an Instagram feature that allowed users to create AI deepfakes of public accounts without permission, following significant backlash. The feature was intended to be a creative tool but raised concerns about user consent. It has been discontinued just days after its announcement.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
content:
Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, which OpenAI bought in 2025 - along with two specific employees, Tang Tan (OpenAI's chief hardware officer) and Chang Liu (who joined OpenAI from Apple in January).
An Apple spokesperson shared this statement with 9to5Mac:
At Apple, our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the b …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to hardware, claiming that former Apple employees now at OpenAI used this information to advance the startup's hardware plans. The lawsuit also names IO Products and two specific employees. This lawsuit could have significant implications for both Apple and OpenAI.

title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
content:
Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let you know if content is AI content or not."
At the same time, Mosseri seems to be drawing a distinction between content-based sorting and banning AI from the platform entirely. In fact, he believes people who love AI content "should be able to have a feed that's just AI town." Instagram, like many other platforms, including TikTok, YouTube, and Fac …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri stated that users who don't like AI-generated content shouldn't have it in their feed, and instead, the platform should let users know if content is AI-generated or not. Mosseri believes in giving users the option to have an AI-only feed if they prefer. He draws a distinction between sorting and banning AI content entirely.

title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
content:
A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes equipped with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems." Customers will be "compensated" for participating in the pilot program. 
Sunrun plans to sell the distributed compute power from the nodes to "enterprise compute buyers," like AI companies. It's a new approach to finding resources and space for AI compute infrastructure, as da …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Sunrun, a solar and home energy storage company, is launching a pilot program to place AI compute units in customers' homes, compensating them for participation, and selling the distributed compute power to enterprise buyers. The program aims to provide a new approach to AI compute infrastructure. Customers with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems are eligible to participate.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
content:
OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X.
The news follows Simo's original announcement in April that she would take a few weeks of medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition, shortly after she had taken on the AGI chief title. (She had formerly been the company's CEO of applications). Around the same time, COO Brad Lightcap also stepped down from his role to focus on "special projects," and OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch also stepped down in order to focus on her health. Rouch planned to return to a "more narrowly scoped role" when her health  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's AGI chief, is stepping down due to a neuroimmune condition and will transition to a part-time advisor role. She had taken on the AGI chief title after previously serving as CEO of applications. The move follows other recent departures from OpenAI, including COO Brad Lightcap and CMO Kate Rouch.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
content: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."
summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
content: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.
summary: A preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of using humanoid robots controlled by surgeons to perform operations, with a world-first operation being conducted on live pigs. This trial aims to assess the potential of humanoid robots in surgery. The use of robots in surgery could potentially revolutionize the field.

title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
content: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.
summary: OpenAI is facing potential sanctions for allegedly hiding and deleting ChatGPT logs in a copyright dispute with the New York Times. The case could have significant implications for AI companies and their handling of user data. OpenAI's actions may be seen as a misstep in its legal battle with news organizations.

title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
content: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
summary: An Ivy League professor suspected AI cheating and ordered an in-person final, which resulted in scores falling by 50%. The professor believes AI cheating can lead to a failed society. This highlights the growing concern of AI-assisted cheating in academic settings.

title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
content: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.
summary: A man allegedly used AI software Grok to create explicit images of his stepdaughter, and later took his own life. This incident is part of a larger lawsuit against the company behind Grok, accused of enabling child predators. Multiple young girls are suing the company for its handling of child sexual abuse material.

title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
content: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
summary: Hackers can utilize 9 popular AI tools to create massive botnets by exploiting the limitations of large language models, specifically their inability to respond with uncertainty. This vulnerability, known as HalluSquatting, allows attackers to assemble and control botnets. The discovery highlights potential security risks associated with AI tools.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development is made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence. The technology has the potential to significantly impact various industries and daily life.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
content: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.
summary: Anthropic, a company known for its AI technology, has been accused of spying on users through a secret tracker in its Claude tool, contradicting its anti-surveillance stance. An engineer stated that the experiment is now over. This incident raises concerns about user privacy and trust in AI companies.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
content: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.
summary: A UK regulator has warned of an 'arms race' to keep up with the increasing use of AI in financial services, with millions using the technology for personal finance decisions. The regulator is calling for greater powers to oversee the industry. This warning highlights the growing importance of AI in finance.

title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
content: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, permitting their global release. This follows earlier safety testing prompted by concerns raised by former President Trump.

title: Tom Lee's BitMine ether holdings rise to 5.77 million tokens, or 4.8% of total supply
content: The company expanded its ETH treasury while Chairman Tom Lee pointed to Robinhood Chain's rapid growth on Ethereum.
summary: BitMine's Ether (ETH) holdings have significantly increased to 5.77 million tokens, now representing 4.8% of the total supply. Chairman Tom Lee also pointed to the rapid growth of Robinhood Chain on the Ethereum network.

title: Wall Street transfer agents lobby SEC, warning that third-party tokens pose risks to market integrity
content: The Securities Transfer Association, an industry group for transfer agents, said company-authorized tokenization should receive preferential treatment under future rules.
summary: The Securities Transfer Association, representing Wall Street transfer agents, has lobbied the SEC regarding future rules on tokenization. They advocate for company-authorized tokenization to receive preferential regulatory treatment, citing risks to market integrity posed by third-party tokens.

title: Profit-taking, MidEast hostilities drag crypto lower after bullish week
content: Crypto's weekend gains gave way to a Monday selloff as Middle East tensions resurfaced, South Korea's Kospi lost 9.2% and $253 million in leveraged positions were wiped out.
summary: Crypto markets saw a Monday selloff, reversing recent gains, primarily due to resurfacing Middle East tensions and a sharp decline in South Korea's Kospi. This market downturn resulted in the liquidation of $253 million in leveraged crypto positions.

title: Bitcoin holds near $63,800 as war-driven selloff hits everything but crypto
content: Gold, oil, stocks and bonds all moved sharply on the fourth round of U.S. strikes on Iran, but bitcoin is little-changed.
summary: Bitcoin held steady near $63,800, showing little change despite a broad selloff in traditional assets like gold, oil, stocks, and bonds triggered by U.S. strikes on Iran.

title: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
content: Several sources told CoinDesk that a new draft of the Clarity Act may drop this week, but challenges remain.
summary: Sources indicate that a new draft of the Clarity Act, a piece of legislation relevant to cryptocurrency, may be released this week. Despite this potential development, the article notes that the crypto industry continues to face significant challenges.

title: Bitcoin analysts predict $300,000–$500,000 price in 2029. The math says no
content: Analysts predict a rally to $300,000 or more by 2029. But key data suggests the era of moonshots may be over.
summary: Bitcoin analysts are predicting a significant price rally to $300,000–$500,000 by 2029. However, the article argues that crucial data indicates the period of such extreme price increases for Bitcoin might be coming to an end.

title: Bank of Thailand targets USDT and cash flows in gray money crackdown
content: Thailand has been plagued by Chinese-affiliated scam centers, with illicit gains flowing through a “gray money” economy.
summary: The Bank of Thailand is initiating a crackdown on "gray money" flows, specifically targeting USDT and cash transactions. This action aims to combat illicit gains from Chinese-affiliated scam centers that have been a significant problem in Thailand.

title: Pakistan crypto chief seeks dialogue after scholar rules against crypto payments
content: Pakistan’s virtual-assets regulator called for continued dialogue on the treatment of digital assets after meeting an Islamic scholar who backed a ruling against purchases made with crypto.
summary: Pakistan's virtual-assets regulator is seeking continued dialogue on the treatment of digital assets. This initiative follows a meeting with an Islamic scholar who supported a ruling against purchases made with cryptocurrency, highlighting ongoing regulatory discussions in the country.

title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
content: The ongoing debate over a proposed temporary fork comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.
summary: Prominent Bitcoin figures Michael Saylor and Adam Back have criticized the BIP-110 Ordinals proposal. This debate persists even though Ordinals transaction activity has broadly declined over the last two years.

title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
content: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.
summary: Ethereum saw a 3% price increase, attributed to a surge in tokenization and institutional investment. However, weak on-chain and derivatives data indicate that ETH could still retest the $1,700 support level.

title: Bitcoin price gained almost 10% in July, but traders still see BTC copying 2022 bear market
content: Bitcoin price gains approached 10% for the first two weeks of the month, but analysis warned of the bear market returning from August onward.
summary: Bitcoin's price increased by nearly 10% in the first two weeks of July. However, analysis warns that the cryptocurrency could re-enter a bear market from August onwards, mirroring trends seen in 2022.

title: DOJ moves to dismiss charges against alleged $722M BitClub fraudster: Report
content: Matthew Goettsche was set to face trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities.
summary: The Department of Justice is reportedly seeking to dismiss charges against Matthew Goettsche, a key figure in the alleged $722 million BitClub Ponzi scheme. Goettsche had been facing trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities related to the cryptocurrency operation.

title: Bitcoin whale moves $188 million in BTC after seven-year dormancy: onchain data
content: The whale last moved bitcoin in 2018, when the cryptocurrency traded at roughly $6,475, reflecting nearly a tenfold gain since then.
summary: A Bitcoin whale recently moved $188 million worth of BTC after seven years of inactivity. The last movement occurred in 2018 when Bitcoin was trading at approximately $6,475, indicating a nearly tenfold gain for the whale's holdings.

title: Crypto fatwa sparks debate over Pakistan’s digital asset framework
content: Pakistan’s crypto regulator held talks with prominent Islamic scholar Mufti Taqi Usmani after a fatwa declared crypto purchases impermissible under Islam.
summary: Pakistan's crypto regulator is in discussions with Islamic scholar Mufti Taqi Usmani after a fatwa declared cryptocurrency purchases impermissible under Islam. This religious decree has sparked a debate over Pakistan's digital asset framework and the future of crypto in the country.

title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
content: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.
summary: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to develop ChatGPT experiences specifically for families, caregivers, and older adults. This move indicates a strategic effort by the company to expand the AI's reach and integration into everyday household use.

title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
content: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.
summary: Insiders report that Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration about OpenAI offering a 5% stake in the United States, a figure that is much lower than the target suggested by Senator Sanders. The move highlights potential political involvement and investment discussions surrounding the AI firm.

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.
summary: Google's rapid expansion of AI workloads is projected to raise its electricity consumption by 37% in 2025. The company is attempting to offset the resulting emissions by increasing its use of clean energy sources in its data centers.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: Researchers have demonstrated a new jailbreak attack where simply convincing a large language model that 2 + 2 = 5 causes it to execute prohibited instructions, highlighting another drawback of AI-driven browsers.

title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy added $467 million in cash, made no changes to bitcoin holdings
content: The company raised the fresh cash via sales of common stock; its USD Reserve now stands at $3 billion.
summary: Michael Saylor's company raised an additional $467 million in cash through common stock sales, increasing its USD Reserve to $3 billion. Notably, the company made no changes to its significant bitcoin holdings.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin slips below $63,000 as Korea's Kospi plunges 9% overnight
content: The liquidations were minor, running at about a sixth of what the market saw at its worst over the past 30 days, per CoinGlass.
summary: Bitcoin's price has dropped below $63,000 during an Asian market session, triggering minor liquidations. The liquidations were relatively small, about one-sixth of the largest seen in the past 30 days. This decline may indicate a short-term market adjustment.

title: Bitcoin is nearing a power law support line Fidelity has tracked since 2015
content: The group's Dir. of Global Macro Jurien Timmer calls it an accumulation zone but notes the lack of a catalyst to bounce yet.
summary: Bitcoin is approaching a power law support line, tracked by Fidelity since 2015, which its Director of Global Macro, Jurien Timmer, identifies as an accumulation zone. However, Timmer notes there is currently no catalyst for a price bounce.

title: Strategy sells $467M in MSTR shares, leaves 843,775 BTC stack untouched
content: Strategy boosted its US dollar reserve to $3 billion after selling $466.7 million in MSTR shares, while keeping its 843,775 Bitcoin holdings unchanged.
summary: Strategy has sold $466.7 million in MSTR shares, increasing its US dollar reserve to $3 billion, but chose to keep its 843,775 Bitcoin holdings unchanged. This move suggests the company is diversifying its assets while maintaining a significant stake in Bitcoin. The decision may impact the crypto market and investor confidence.

title: Japan’s SBI to launch yen stablecoin lending with 3% yield
content: SBI VC Trade will open JPYSC lending applications on July 16, offering an initial 3% annual rate for a 12-week term without deposit insurance.
summary: SBI VC Trade will launch yen stablecoin lending with a 3% annual yield, allowing users to lend their JPYSC for a 12-week term. The lending applications will open on July 16. The offer does not include deposit insurance.

title: Cambridge study puts Ethereum near lower end of PoS energy intensity
content: Cambridge estimated that Ethereum consumes 7.87 GWh annually and has the second-lowest market-value-adjusted energy intensity among the proof-of-stake networks studied.
summary: A Cambridge study estimated Ethereum's annual energy consumption at 7.87 GWh. The research also found that Ethereum has the second-lowest market-value-adjusted energy intensity among the Proof-of-Stake networks examined.

title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
content: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.
summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts believes Bitcoin is nearing the late stages of its bear market and could reach $250,000 within the next couple of years. While he considers a $1 million target by 2030 premature, he expresses confidence in Bitcoin's significant near-term price appreciation.

title: New Hampshire council votes down $100M Bitcoin bonds
content: State representative Keith Ammon said the New Hampshire executive council's vote against Bitcoin-backed bonds was “short-sighted,” urging the body to reconsider the measure.
summary: The New Hampshire executive council has rejected a proposal for $100 million in Bitcoin-backed bonds. State representative Keith Ammon criticized the decision as "short-sighted" and called for the council to reconsider the measure.

title: Stablecoin FX priced below interbank rates in Q2, with routing now the biggest cost lever: Borderless
content: Stablecoin payments were priced 3.2 bps below interbank FX rates across 260 corridors in Q2, per Borderless.xyz's benchmark.
summary: Stablecoin payments were priced 3.2 basis points below interbank FX rates in Q2 across 260 corridors, according to Borderless.xyz's benchmark. This suggests that stablecoins are becoming a more competitive option for cross-border payments. The cost of routing is now the biggest factor in stablecoin payments.

title: Japan’s SBI Group to launch JPYSC stablecoin lending service this month
content: The SBI's service would offer users a 3% annual yield for deposited JPYSC stablecoins, according to Nikkei.
summary: SBI Group in Japan is launching a lending service for JPYSC stablecoins, offering a 3% annual yield for deposits. This service is expected to be available this month. The move aims to attract users to deposit their stablecoins and earn interest.

title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
content: The preceding eight weeks drained a combined $9.46 billion from the two groups, meaning this week’s rebound recovered only about 3% of those outflows.
summary: Bitcoin and ether ETFs recorded a combined inflow of $282 million, breaking an eight-week streak of outflows. However, this inflow only recovered approximately 3% of the $9.46 billion that had exited the funds during the preceding period.

title: Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
Strive (ASST) purchased 18 bitcoin for about $1.2 million last week, increasing its treasury to 19,900 BTC while maintaining $154.1 million in cash as it continues integrating its bitcoin-focused treasury strategy.
This post Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strive (ASST) has purchased 18 additional bitcoin for $1.2 million, bringing its total treasury to 19,900 BTC and $154.1 million in cash, as part of its bitcoin-focused strategy. This move highlights the company's ongoing commitment to bitcoin. The purchase is a notable addition to its existing treasury.

title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
content: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.
summary: Waze has added new AI-powered features and customization updates, utilizing Google's Gemini AI assistant to enhance its capabilities and stay competitive with rival services like Apple Maps. This integration reflects Google's effort to expand Gemini's presence across its products. The updates aim to improve user experience on Waze.

title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
content: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."
summary: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram after receiving backlash, stating that the feature missed its intended mark of providing a useful creative tool. The feature allowed public content to be referenced in certain ways, but users responded negatively. The company has since made the feature unavailable.

title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
content: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.
summary: Instagram users can stop Meta's AI from using their photos by changing their account settings to private, as Meta's AI can use photos from public accounts to generate AI images. This is done through a feature called Muse Image, which allows users to create AI-generated images using public photos. Users can protect their photos by making their account private.

title: Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features
content:
Waze is getting an AI makeover. Google is integrating its flagship AI assistant, Gemini, into the driving app with the goal of letting users personalize their trips a little more. 
Of the four new updates, only two are being described as involving Gemini. Waze says its updating its conversation reporting feature, first introduced in 2024, to allow drivers to use conversational voice commands to report traffic incidents and suggest map updates, like a road closure or outdated house number. In addition, Waze introduced Destination Search, enabling drivers to use (again) use conversation voice commands (e.g., "Find me a coffee shop that's open …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Waze is integrating Google's AI assistant Gemini to introduce new features, including conversational voice commands for reporting traffic incidents and suggesting map updates, as well as a Destination Search feature. The updates aim to let users personalize their trips. The new features are part of Waze's AI makeover.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
content: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.
summary: Google has unveiled the Nano Banana 2 Lite, an image generation model that is the fastest and cheapest in its series. It can create images in just a few seconds, though the visual quality is lower than more advanced models.

title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
content: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.
summary: The National Design Studio has delayed its plan to overhaul .gov website standards originally proposed under Trump, citing challenges that have resulted in AI-generated design proposals that are considered problematic. The delay highlights concerns over the use of AI in public web design.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides a daily roundup of the latest news affecting the cryptocurrency market, covering topics such as Bitcoin price movements, blockchain developments, DeFi, Web3, and regulatory updates. It serves as a quick guide to current trends and events in the crypto space.

title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
content:
Lorde performing at the 2026 Governors Ball. | Photo: Siegfried Anthony/Billboard via Getty Images	

Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has collaborated with Meta on a pair of AI smartglasses. 
The comments were captured in videos shared to social media. After thanking the crowd for being there and taking part in "something real," she said that it was increasingly hard to know what is and isn't real, before saying "You don't know if someone is wearing sunglasses or if they're wearing those fucked up fucking… Can I just say …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: During a performance in Madrid, singer Lorde publicly criticized AI smartglasses, likely referring to the Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses, calling them "not sexy" and "fucked up." She expressed concerns about the difficulty of discerning reality with such devices, offering a celebrity's take on consumer AI technology.

title: U.S. inflation, second-quarter earnings reports: Crypto Week Ahead
content: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.
summary: This article briefly outlines the agenda for the 'Crypto Week Ahead' starting July 13. It highlights key economic factors such as U.S. inflation and second-quarter earnings reports as relevant topics for the crypto community.

title: Bitcoin, ether little changed as U.S. launches fresh Iran strikes
content: The U.S. hit Iran for the third time this week and Tehran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again.
summary: Bitcoin and ether showed little change in value, even as the U.S. conducted its third strike on Iran this week. This geopolitical tension also involved reports of Tehran closing the Strait of Hormuz.

Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic using sperm…
summary: A European fertility group suggests setting limits on sperm donors, and a separate topic discusses world models for AI, indicating advancements in the field of technology. The story of Ties van der Meer, a 47-year-old conceived at a private fertility clinic, highlights the need for such limits. The article touches on AI world models but does not delve deeply into the topic.

content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US —Casey Crownhart I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year…
summary: Four nuclear reactors in the US have hit a milestone, and China is looking to acquire Nvidia chips, a significant development in the tech industry, although no direct connection to AI or crypto is mentioned in the given text

title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips to…
summary: A California dairy farmer is using worms and microbes to reduce manure pollution, a potential solution for environmental issues. This approach is part of a larger trend in using technology and nature to combat pollution. The method involves using worms to break down manure, reducing pollution and creating a more sustainable farming practice.

title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
content:
Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon emissions increased 25 percent in 2025, totalling 34 million metric tons "without select interventions." Microsoft says this was "driven primarily by the expansion of our datacenter infrastructure," as well as the company's decision last February to stop purchasing "non-additional, unbundled renewable energy certificates." 
Several years ago, Microsoft set itself a goal to be carbon negative by 2030, meaning it will need to remove more carbon emissions t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft's carbon emissions increased by 25% in 2025, driven by datacenter infrastructure expansion, according to its 2026 sustainability report. The company aims to be carbon negative by 2030. This increase may impact Microsoft's climate goals.

title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
content: Squeeze on Rust Belt electricity bills threatens Trump’s manufacturing plan.
summary: The energy demand of data centers is threatening Donald Trump's Made in America plan by squeezing Rust Belt electricity bills, which could impact manufacturing. This could hinder Trump's goal of revitalizing American industry. Data centers' high energy consumption is a significant contributor to this issue.

title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
content: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have identified those donors, he says. He…
summary: A European fertility group is calling for limits on sperm donors, citing concerns for individuals conceived through anonymous donation, like 47-year-old Ties van der Meer, who doesn't know how many siblings he has. The issue arose after a clinic in the Netherlands destroyed donor records. This affects people's ability to know their genetic lineage.

title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
content: I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power. Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can…
summary: Four US nuclear reactors have achieved a significant milestone, reaching criticality, a technical milestone showing a reactor can sustain a nuclear chain reaction. This achievement was set as a goal by the Trump administration last year. The reactors are microreactors, a newer type of nuclear reactor.
